Policy_Statement: All AI models must be registered in the centralized model registry before production deployment. Model cards must include: owner, version, training data source, performance metrics, known limitations, and decommission date.

AI training and inference data must comply with enterprise Data Governance policies. PII/PHI data requires explicit consent documentation and privacy impact assessment. Data lineage must be tracked end-to-end.
All AI workloads must use approved cloud services via Infrastructure-as-Code (Terraform on GCP, CDK/Terraform on AWS). Direct API access for production workloads is prohibited.

Generative AI tools used in the enterprise must be approved for the data classification level handled. Confidential data must not be sent to unapproved public endpoints (e.g., consumer ChatGPT). Enterprise-managed GenAI is the default.
Third-party AI services must undergo security, performance, and compliance evaluation before integration. Vendor must provide SOC 2 Type II, penetration test results, and data processing agreements.
AI workloads must have defined budgets, alert thresholds at 50/75/100%, and chargeback mechanisms. Preemptible/Spot instances required for training workloads where possible.
AI-specific incidents (model failures, bias incidents, prompt injection) must follow the AI Incident Runbook. All incidents severity P1/P2 require EA notification within 1 hour.
All significant AI architecture decisions must be documented as ADRs and stored in the Architecture Repository. ADRs require peer review by at least one other architect and the Domain Architect.
